The agreement introduces the automatic exchange of country-by-country reports, a key OECD tool designed to help governments detect profit shifting and other aggressive […] The post How Morocco's new tax deal could make it harder for multinationals to move profits to low-tax countries appeared first on HESPRESS English - Morocco News .

articles.title: Four sites in Bushehr targeted by US strikes, Iranian official says
articles.summary: Four sites in Bushehr targeted by US strikes, Iranian official says US air strikes hit four locations in the southern Iranian city of Bushehr, Iran’s official news agency IRNA reported, quoting the province’s deputy governor. Iranian officials also said that US strikes targeted the cities of Abadan and Mahshahr in Khuzestan province.  The attacks struck the two cities on Monday afternoon, the report quoted Khuzestan Deputy Governor Valiollah Hayati as saying.  Abadan is home to the Middle East’s oldest oil refinery, while Mahshahr is a key port city on Iran’s Gulf coast.

The country's foreign ministry, in a statement, said that the IRGC was an official part of Iran’s armed forces and accused London of violating international law by targeting a state institution. articles.title: Israel to hold national elections on 27 October, Knesset says
articles.summary: Israel to hold national elections on 27 October, Knesset says Israel’s parliament will dissolve on 17 July and national elections will be held on 27 October as scheduled, according to the Knesset's legal adviser Sagit Afik. Times of Israel reported that the vote would mark Israel’s first election held on schedule since 1988 and make Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s government the first Israeli administration to complete a full term since 1973. The current coalition was formed in December 2022 following the collapse of the Naftali Bennett's Yair Lapid government.

articles.title: GCC condemns attacks on Emirati tankers in Hormuz
articles.summary: GCC condemns attacks on Emirati tankers in Hormuz The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) has condemned attacks on two Emirati oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, with Secretary-General Jasem Mohamed Albudaiwi describing the incident as an “explicit violation” of UN Security Council Resolution 2817, which calls for the protection of international navigation. Albudaiwi said the GCC stands firmly with the United Arab Emirates and urged the international community, particularly the UN Security Council, to take decisive action to halt further attacks. articles.title: Another vessel attacked near Oman, UK maritime body says
articles.summary: Another vessel attacked near Oman, UK maritime body says An oil tanker was targeted by a missile while transiting the southern route of the Strait of Hormuz near Limah, Oman, the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency said, adding that the vessel was struck about 13 nautical miles east of the Omani town. The incident follows a separate attack reported a day earlier near Qalhat, where UKMTO said a tanker was hit by an unknown projectile on the starboard-side engine room. All crew members aboard the Qalhat vessel were reported safe, and no environmental damage was recorded. UKMTO WARNING 08

articles.title: Qatar backs Saudi Arabia after missile attack by Houthis
articles.summary: Qatar backs Saudi Arabia after missile attack by Houthis Qatar has strongly condemned a missile attack on Saudi Arabia’s southern region, describing it as a violation of the kingdom’s sovereignty and territorial integrity and a threat to regional security. In a statement, Qatar’s foreign ministry reiterated its full support for Saudi Arabia and stressed that attacks targeting the kingdom undermine stability in the Gulf.
